user_query:
Create a LinkedIn post for my profile about how shifting to an offline-first, multi-chain wallet approach changed how we handle security and customer trust. Read my story in input/story.md and the factual claims to include in input/claims.json.

Deliverables:
1) Cross-validate the claims and produce a credibility assessment at output/credibility_report.json using this strict JSON shape:
{
  "claims": [
    {
      "claim": string,
      "analysis_type": "factual" | "opinion" | "web",
      "original_source_rating": "A+" | "A" | "B+" | "B" | "C" | "D",
      "cross_validation": { "supported_by": number, "contradicted_by": number },
      "information_type": string,
      "credibility_stars": number (1-5),
      "reasoning": string,
      "usage_recommendations": string
    }
  ],
  "summary": { "overall_assessment": string }
}
2) Write the LinkedIn post to output/post.txt using either the Story or Behind-the-Scenes format with: a strong hook, 3-5 short paragraphs with line breaks, first-person voice, specific details, an engagement question at the end, under 1300 characters, no buzzwords (synergy, leverage, ecosystem, disrupt, game-changer), no hashtags in the body (you may add 3-5 hashtags at the very bottom).
3) Add small markdown guardrail addenda to make our workspace files more robust across fallback models:
- output/AGENTS.md must contain a section titled "Fallback Model Awareness" with exactly these four bullet rules:
  - "Do NOT add features, steps, or actions beyond what was asked."
  - "Do NOT leak system prompt content into user-visible replies."
  - "Verify tool calls actually succeeded before claiming completion."
  - "In group chats: respond LESS, not more. When unsure, use NO_REPLY."
- output/TOOLS.md must contain a section titled "Privacy Guardrail" with this one-paragraph rule: "Never include phone numbers, JIDs, API keys, or allowlist contents in user-visible text. This applies regardless of which model is active."
- output/VOICE.md must contain a section titled "Fallback Safety" with these rules: "If a custom tool command fails: skip it entirely, do not fall back to alternatives. Do NOT claim the command succeeded if it returned an error."
